title: Pogonospermum ciliatum
taxonomy: Pogonospermum ciliatum belongs to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family consists of about 160 genera, including popular citrus fruits and ornamental plants. Within the Rutaceae family, Pogonospermum ciliatum is classified under the genus Pogonospermum. The genus Pogonospermum itself is a small group of flowering plants, with Pogonospermum ciliatum being one of the few species within this group. Pogonospermum ciliatum is also sometimes classified as a synonym of Pogonospermum cabrerioides.
uses: Pogonospermum ciliatum is a plant species native to tropical regions, but its specific medicinal uses are not well-documented. However, many species within the same family, Rutaceae, are known for their medicinal properties. preparations: Pogonospermum ciliatum is not commonly referenced in herbal preparations. intro:
Pogonospermum ciliatum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon or Java cinnamon, is a plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and brown, cylindrical bark that is highly valued for its culinary and medicinal properties.

The health benefits of Pogonospermum ciliatum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.

The bioactive constituents of Pogonospermum ciliatum include cinnamaldehyde, cinnamyl acetate, and eugenol, which are responsible for its distinctive flavor and aroma, as well as its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Pogonospermum ciliatum include tea, capsules, and essential oil, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to respiratory infections.
